codicology.animal_note: Young cattle vellum typical for Icelandic manuscripts of this period; fine grain and moderate opacity consistent with calfskin.
codicology.region_note: Inferred from scribal hand, parchment preparation quality, and Icelandic transmission context.
codicology.age_consensus: Standard scholarly consensus (Árni Magnússon Institute; handrit.is)

preparation.finish: Well-prepared, relatively thin sheets for a large composite codex

timeline.event: Oral and written Eddic tradition circulating in Iceland/Norway
Probable terminus post quem for exemplar traditions behind Codex Regius
Main scribe copies Poetic Edda onto prepared vellum
Marginalia and minor corrections (same or secondary hand — pending page-level analysis)
Probable terminus ante quem for primary copying phase
Bishop Brynjólfur Sveinsson presents codex to Frederick III of Denmark; named Codex Regius
Returned to Iceland; Árni Magnússon Institute stewardship

citations: Neckel & Kuhn 1983: Gustav Neckel and Hans Kuhn, *Edda: Die Lieder des Codex Regius*, 2 vols., Heidelberg: Carl Winter, 1983. <https://archive.org/details/edda00neck>
handrit.is: Digital manuscript viewer for GKS 2365 4to. <https://handrit.is/manuscript/view/is/GKS04-2365/9>
Árni Magnússon Institute: Stofnun Árna Magnússonar í íslenskum fræðum, Reykjavík. <https://arnastofnun.is/>
